content: Individual participations only.
Each battle features two dancers facing off in a head-to-head challenge.
Battle Format: Each round, the music genre changes unpredictably. Possible styles include Rap, Pop, Break beats, Funk, Electronica, House, Bollywood etc.
Time per dancer: 45–60 seconds to showcase their moves before the opponent responds.
Battles are conducted in a knockout format, leading to a Final Showdown.
Judges select one dancer from each battle to advance to the next round.
The knockout rounds continue until only two dancers remain for the final showdown.
Final Showdown: The last two dancers compete in multiple rounds with surprise music transitions.
Teams must consist of a minimum of 4 and a maximum of 15 members.
All members of the team should individually register for the event
Performance duration: 5+2 minutes (including stage setup and clearance).
Dance styles encompass both Eastern and Western forms without separate categories.
Teams must showcase their creativity and versatility within the given time frame.
Use of props is allowed but should not cause stage damage.
Any form of inappropriate content or offensive gestures will lead to disqualification.
Individual participations only.

Match Format: Knockout matches : 2–3 overs per side depending on registration.

Each team shall consist of 8 players.
Overs & Bowling Rule (1-1-1): Each bowler can bowl only 1 over.
All 8 players can bat. If 7 wickets fall, last batter may not continue.
Boundary will be marked within 30 yards.
Wide and No-ball will give 1 extra run + extra ball.
The umpire's decision is final and binding.
In case of tie: Super Over (1 over each); Higher boundary count if Super Over not possible.
Misconduct or arguments with umpire may lead to disqualification.

The Guide must not touch the keyboard, mouse or screen at any time.

Each player will get 1 minute 30 seconds to play.
Once the round begins, the game cannot be paused or stopped.
The player must choose one car before the round begins and cannot change it during gameplay.
Select either Manual or Automatic mode before the round begins; cannot change during the game.
For every collision, 1 KM will be deducted from the total distance covered.
Final Score = Total Distance Covered - Total Penalty.
Rankings will be decided based on the final recorded score after penalty deductions.
Each participant is allowed one regular attempt.
Participants who wish to improve their score may pay the same registration fee for one additional round; best score counts.

All members of the team should individually register for the event
Movies will be selected only from English, Kannada and Hindi.
Each team will be timed during their turn.
The team that correctly guesses the movie in the shortest time will be declared the winner.
